 However I believe you are referring to Mentha x piperita f. citrata 'mentha triptans'  is not a known botanical name but  I believe you are actually referring to Mentha x piperita f. citrata 'mentha triptans' is not a known botanical name but Mentha pulegium is a known botanical name also known as  Pennyroyal is a perennial herb that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has a long history of use in traditional medicine and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mentha pulegium include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of Mentha pulegium include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
